response_text: This is false. Rust does not have a garbage collector. Instead, Rust uses a system of ownership with rules that the compiler checks at compile time. This system includes concepts like ownership, borrowing, and lifetimes to manage memory safely and efficiently without needing a garbage collector. The ownership system ensures that memory is automatically freed when it is no longer needed, typically at the end of a variable's scope. This approach allows Rust to provide memory safety guarantees and high performance similar to languages with manual memory management, like C and C++.
